title: Black Forest cake
subtitle: Chocolate sponge layered with kirsch cream and cherries
excerpt: A two-layer chocolate sponge soaked in kirsch syrup, filled and covered with whipped cream and glacé cherries, finished with dark chocolate shavings. The kirsch syrup is the defining element: without it the sponge is dry. Refrigerate before serving and eat within two days.
sourceNotes: The Schwarzwälder Kirschtorte (Black Forest cherry gateau) is a German cake documented from the late nineteenth century. German domestic cookbooks of the nineteenth and early twentieth century, available through the Internet Archive, document a chocolate sponge layered with whipped cream and kirsch-soaked cherries. The cake appears in English-language European cooking references including domestic baking manuals of the mid-twentieth century. The key elements (chocolate sponge, kirsch syrup, whipped cream, cherries, and chocolate shavings) are consistently documented across European public-domain sources.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble the cake and refrigerate for up to 24 hours before serving. The sponge layers can be baked 2 days ahead, wrapped, and stored at room temperature.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf. Bake one layer at a time if you have only one tin

glossaryTerms.definition: A simple syrup (equal weights of sugar and water, simmered briefly) combined with kirsch and brushed generously over sponge cake layers. The syrup moistens and flavours the sponge, which would otherwise dry out under a cream filling. For Black Forest cake the ratio is typically two parts syrup to one part kirsch; adjust to taste but do not omit.

content.text: The kirsch syrup is not optional. A plain chocolate sponge under a layer of cream will dry the mouth; the syrup-soaked version stays moist and picks up the cherry-brandy flavour that defines this cake. Brush the syrup on generously while the sponge is still warm, so it absorbs fully rather than pooling on the surface.

items.prepNote: halved; fresh or jarred sour cherries if available

content.text: Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Butter and line the tin. Whisk the {{eggs}} and the {{caster-sugar}} for the sponge until pale and thickened, about 3 minutes. Whisk in the melted {{unsalted-butter}} and {{whole-milk}}. Fold in the sifted {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Divide the batter evenly between two batches (bake one layer, then use the same tin for the second). Bake each layer for 25–28 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool on a rack.
Dissolve the {{caster-sugar}} for the syrup in the {{water}} in a small saucepan over medium heat. Simmer for 2 minutes, remove from the heat, and stir in the {{kirsch}}. This is the
. Brush it generously over both sponge layers while they are still warm — use all of it.

Whip the {{double-cream}} with the {{caster-sugar}} for the cream and {{vanilla-extract}} to soft peaks. Spread half the cream over the bottom sponge layer. Scatter half the {{glace-cherries}} over the cream. Place the second sponge on top. Spread the remaining cream over the top and sides. Scatter the remaining cherries over the surface and press the {{dark-chocolate}} shavings around the sides and across the top. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.
